mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 6.0 percent, it signals the exhaustion of cyclical labor market slack. This threshold breach historically precedes wage-driven consumption acceleration, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or zero forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances across a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in residential housing starts and consumer credit balances indicates synchronized household demand expansion. This joint signal captures broad-based spending momentum that directly lifts corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of historical instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production directly reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side throughput. In a post-shock recovery regime, this supply expansion correlates with positive earnings revisions, generating positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with INDPRO growth rates over a 3-year rolling window (correlation coefficient < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth accelerating above 2.5 percent signals robust aggregate demand expansion. This threshold breach confirms corporate profit growth acceleration, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 55% of instances where quarterly GDP growth exceeds 2.5% over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation tightening regime, rising CPI combined with rising policy rates compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and margin compression. This dual pressure systematically reduces forward equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 60-day forward returns in this regime are not significantly negative (mean return > -0.5%) or if the joint series show no negative correlation with equity returns over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll growth reflects continuous economic expansion and stable corporate revenue streams. This steady labor market input provides a persistent positive drift to equity markets over weekly horizons without requiring threshold crossings.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns show no positive monotonic relationship with PAYEMS growth rates, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 50% of positive payroll growth months over a 3-year test.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts crossing 1000 thousand units while unemployment drops below 6.5 percent confirms a broad-based demand recovery. This joint threshold breach triggers positive equity returns by validating synchronized consumer and business confidence improvements.
candidate_trigger: HOUST exceeds 1000 and UNRATE falls below 6.5
failure_condition: The joint trigger fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of historical instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.
